React, You'll Learn It Eventually — Why Not Now? - Setting Up React with Vite - (22)
React, You'll Learn It Eventually — Why Not Now?

This is where we kick off a whole new chapter: Vite. A lot of you have probably already used Vite on a project, and if you haven't, no worries — I'll walk you through why Vite is so nice to work with, and how to actually use it. We'll build a Vite-for-React project from scratch, poke at its folder structure, and wire up Tailwind CSS along the way.
Making VSCode's Tailwind CSS IntelliSense Work With React JSX
Tailwind CSS IntelliSense worked fine in a CRA (Create React App) setup, but for some reason after I moved to Vite for React it stopped working. This post is just a note on how I fixed it.

Tailwind CSS is a utility-first CSS framework with a lot of upsides, which is why so many people are picking it up. But in VSCode you often get "Unknown at rule @tailwind" or "Unknown at rule @apply" warnings — here's how to silence them.
